article_title: FreeBSD 15.1 Ships With Wi-Fi Drivers Rebased on Linux 7.0, Boot-Time Scheduler Selection, and Unicode 17
summary: Submission approvable with 1 minor warning(s) — file a corrections record on merge

editor_notes.content_quality: Clean, well-structured News brief (473 words, within the 400-1200 News range). Overview/What We Know/What We Don't Know format is appropriate. Technical depth is accurate and accessible (LinuxKPI explained correctly as a Linux-driver compatibility layer).
editor_notes.source_verification: All 4 source snapshots read from disk and sha256-verified against manifest. source-0 (freebsd.org announce, 200): confirms June 16 2026 announcement date, 'second release of the stable/15 branch', and all five direct quotes verbatim (iwlwifi/LinuxKPI Linux v7.0; kern.sched.name tunable; Unicode 17.0.0/CLDR 48/4,803 characters; C23 progress; cloud images pkg(8)/first-boot updates). source-1 (freebsd.org relnotes, 200): confirms RISC-V iwlwifi LinuxKPI enablement, GENERIC amd64 includes both SCHED_ULE and SCHED_4BSD with boot-time choice, '4 new scripts', OpenSSL 3.5.6, OpenZFS 2.4.2 — version specifics correctly cited to the release notes per Rule 9. source-2 (opensourcefeed.org, 200): confirms the iwlwifi/LinuxKPI rebase to Linux v7.0 attribution. source-3 (freebsd-announce mailing list, 200): confirms six architectures (amd64, aarch64, armv7, powerpc64, powerpc64le, riscv64) and support through March 31 2027. Phoronix is not cited. No orphan URLs — all 4 body links appear in article.sources.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: Quotes verbatim and version specifics all trace to the correct sources. One factual error: the article calls Colin Percival 'the project's Release Engineering Lead', but both the announce page and mailing-list roster identify him as Release Engineering DEPUTY Lead (Ed Maste is the Release Engineering Lead). This is a single recoverable misattribution in a subordinate body sentence, not in the headline/summary/lead thesis — handled via corrections record.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral and professional. No sensationalism, no AI self-reference, no editorializing.
editor_notes.originality: Original. No prior FreeBSD 15.1 release coverage in the archive; the only adjacent open-source/OS pieces cover unrelated topics (Zig, Bun, GDB, governance).
concerns: Article describes Colin Percival as 'the project's Release Engineering Lead'; sources identify him as Release Engineering Deputy Lead (Ed Maste is the Lead). Filed as a correction.
recommendations: All four cited domains (freebsd.org, lists.freebsd.org, opensourcefeed.org) are reputable but not in config/source_allowlist.txt; the primary freebsd.org/lists.freebsd.org domains are the authoritative first-party project sources and warrant being added to the allowlist. Non-blocking — allowlist not modified during this review.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: Accurate, well-sourced News brief on the FreeBSD 15.1 release. All quotes and version specifics verify against first-party snapshots. APPROVE_WITH_CORRECTIONS solely to publicly note that Colin Percival is the Release Engineering Deputy Lead, not the Lead.

content_preview.title: FreeBSD 15.1 Ships With Wi-Fi Drivers Rebased on Linux 7.0, Boot-Time Scheduler Selection, and Unicode 17
content_preview.summary: FreeBSD 15.1-RELEASE arrived June 16, 2026 with LinuxKPI wireless drivers tracking Linux 7.0, a tunable to pick the kernel scheduler at boot, and Unicode 17 support.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

The FreeBSD Project released FreeBSD 15.1-RELEASE on June 16, 2026, the second release of the stable/15 branch, according to the [FreeBSD Project](https://www.freebsd.org/releases/15.1R/announce/). The release was announced by Colin Percival, the project's Release Engineering Lead, on the [freebsd-announce mailing list](https://lists.freebsd.org/archives/freebsd-announce/2026-June/000274.html). It headlines updated wireless networking drivers tracking the Linux kernel, a new mechani...

recommendations: Before merging, the human reviewer should write a corrections file at src/content/corrections/<YYYY-MM>/<article-slug>.json documenting the warning(s).
Consider adding trusted domains to config/source_allowlist.txt
